title: SEO Agent Output
description: Standard structured output contract for every specialist agent in the World-Class SEO Agent System.

agent.description: Exact agent name responsible for this output.

summary.description: Concise summary of the agent's conclusion.

evidence.description: Evidence sources used to support the findings.

source.description: URL, file, export, report, crawl, API, or user-provided source.

type.description: Evidence tier or data type, such as first_party_data, rendered_dom, official_guidance, crawl_export, serp_snapshot, or backlink_data.

date_checked.description: Date the evidence was checked, preferably YYYY-MM-DD.

notes.description: Short note explaining relevance, limitations, or confidence.

confidence.description: Confidence level based on evidence quality.

findings.description: Structured findings with evidence, severity, and affected scope.

severity.description: Severity based on impact and risk.

finding.description: What is wrong or what opportunity exists.

affected_scope.description: URLs, templates, sections, markets, or workflows affected.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence items by source or short label.

recommended_actions.description: Structured actions downstream agents or humans can execute.

owner.description: Agent or team responsible for execution.

success_metric.description: Metric or observable condition proving the action worked.

impact.description: Expected SEO, UX, conversion, risk-reduction, or operational impact.

risks.description: Known risks, tradeoffs, or failure modes.

owner.description: Primary owner for the next step.

dependencies.description: Data, approvals, agents, teams, or systems required before completion.

acceptance_criteria.description: Conditions that must be true for this work to be accepted.

verification.description: Checks that prove the recommendation was correctly implemented or validated.

examples.summary: The product template emits conflicting canonical and hreflang signals.

evidence.notes: Sample of 50 product URLs showed canonical conflicts.

findings.finding: Localized product pages canonicalize to the US page while also declaring hreflang alternates.

recommended_actions.action: Update localized product template so each locale self-canonicalizes and hreflang alternates remain reciprocal.
recommended_actions.success_metric: Sampled localized URLs return self-canonical plus reciprocal hreflang.

examples.impact: Improves international targeting and reduces duplicate/incorrect canonical consolidation risk.
risks: Incorrect rollout could affect indexation across all localized product pages.
acceptance_criteria: Self-canonical is present on sampled localized URLs
No unexpected noindex or robots blocks
verification: Run hreflang audit on sample URLs
Submit sample URLs for indexation validation when available
